full_content:
Verbatim owner statement:
"All cross-cutting technical requirements specifications must be tracked and mechanically enforced (or as close as we can get) for all implementation proposals."

Decision (per owner clarification: Option C):
Two-layer mechanical enforcement, defense in depth:

LAYER 1 - Write-time PreToolUse block (fast-feedback):
- Extends existing formal-artifact-approval-gate.py PreToolUse hook on Write/Edit of bridge/ files.
- Parses bridge proposal text for Specification Links section + cited paths + cited code targets.
- Runs each registered cross-cutting requirement's "fast check" (regex, glob, AST query, or scripted check) against the proposal.
- Hard-blocks the Write/Edit if any fast check reports violation, with the violation reason in the block message.
- Catches obvious violations instantly at proposal-creation time.

LAYER 2 - Codex review-time block (slow-but-thorough):
- Codex review skill loads the registered cross-cutting requirements.
- For each NEW or REVISED bridge entry, Codex runs each requirement's "deep check" (broader text analysis, KB queries, cross-reference validation, contextual judgment) against the proposal text + cited code paths + project state.
- Codex auto-NO-GO's any proposal that violates a deep check.
- Catches subtle/contextual violations the fast check would miss.

REGISTRY:
- Cross-cutting requirements live in a dedicated location (proposed: groundtruth.db spec table with type='cross_cutting_requirement' OR a tracked file at .claude/rules/cross-cutting-requirements.toml).
- Each registered requirement carries: id, title, rule text (verbatim from CLAUDE.md / .claude/rules/*), source DELIB / GOV / DCL ID, fast_check, deep_check, exceptions (if any, with their authorizing DELIB IDs).

CHARTER FOR INITIAL POPULATION:
- Existing rule files (CLAUDE.md "Mandatory Project Root Boundary", .claude/rules/project-root-boundary.md, canonical-terminology.md, loyal-opposition.md, file-bridge-protocol.md Mandatory Specification Linkage Gate, etc.) get migrated to the registry as the FIRST cross-cutting requirements.
- Each gets fast + deep check definitions written.
- The Slice 8.6 incident's project-root-boundary violation would have been blocked by this enforcement.

Rationale:
Slice 8.6 demonstrated the failure mode: rules existed in CLAUDE.md + .claude/rules/project-root-boundary.md but were enforced only by Prime Builder's voluntary compliance. Prime Builder failed to comply, and Codex review didn't catch it because the rule wasn't in Codex's review checklist. Multi-layer mechanical enforcement closes both gaps.

Implementation references:
- Cross-cuts with DELIB-S330-SPEC-CAPTURE-TRANSPARENCY (this requirement itself becomes a registered cross-cutting requirement; its enforcement is itself transparently surfaced).
- Cross-cuts with the requirements-collection hook (Learning 2): classifications produced by that hook feed candidate registrations here.

Owner approval: Approve as drafted (S330 chat, 2026-05-04, via AskUserQuestion).
explicit_change_request: Owner approved insertion via AskUserQuestion: "Approve as drafted". Verbatim source statement: "All cross-cutting technical requirements specifications must be tracked and mechanically enforced (or as close as we can get) for all implementation proposals...."
change_reason: S330 capture queue item approved by owner via AskUserQuestion in chat. Capture per just-approved DELIB-S330-SPEC-CAPTURE-TRANSPARENCY transparency rule.
